summary: Scope-limited testing of the empty-DB + first-user signup + remove demo/Google iteration. All three user-requested items VERIFIED PASSING on backend and frontend. 8/8 pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_signup_flow.py. Playwright sweep across phases (auto-signup-mode when DB empty, signup creates leadership user with httpOnly cookies + /home redirect, signup-allowed flips to false, second signup 403s, fresh login page defaults to 'Welcome back' with no switch-to-signup link, sign-in works, sidebar-home-link present, empty states render) all green. Test user cleaned up at the end — DB is back to empty, signup-allowed=true.

minor.issue: Pydantic EmailStr rejects '.local' TLDs (email-validator flags special-use/reserved). The user's SUGGESTED test creds 'first@studio.local' FAIL with HTTP 422 'value is not a valid email address: The part after the @-sign is a special-use or reserved name'. Switched test email to 'first@studio.com' to verify flow. Recommend either (a) accepting common dev TLDs by configuring email-validator with allow_special_use=True/check_deliverability=False, or (b) updating /app/memory/test_credentials.md to use a real-TLD email.

action_items: (Minor) Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md to suggest a public-TLD email (e.g. first@studio.com) — the currently documented 'first@studio.local' will be rejected by EmailStr.

(Optional) If '.local' (or other RFC-2606/special-use TLDs) should be supported, configure pydantic's EmailStr / email-validator with check_deliverability=False and allow_special_use=True, or switch to a plain str field with a custom validator.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py L313-315 — signup is locked by counting users>0. This is correct, but be aware: in a race two parallel POST /auth/signup could both pass the count check before either inserts. The unique index on users.email (L1042) provides a partial guard for duplicate-email, but a second different-email signup could still slip through. For a single-tenant first-user flow this is acceptable; document the assumption or wrap in a transaction.

/app/frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx L26 — useEffect catches the signup-allowed fetch failure and silently sets signupAllowed=false. If the backend is briefly unreachable, the UI will hide the first-user signup CTA. Consider a retry or an explicit 'unable to check status' notice. Low-priority since the page auto-switches as soon as it loads successfully.

/app/frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx L107 — when signupAllowed becomes false while the user has manually switched to signup mode, fields are disabled but the form still renders. Minor: could redirect to signin or hide the form entirely.

/app/backend/server.py — SignupIn model (not shown) presumably has no password length validation surfaced; the spec asks for 8+ chars but no server-side enforcement was observed beyond what the frontend placeholder hints at. Recommend min_length=8 on the password field for hardening.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all 6 review_request scenarios (empty DB → signup form, demo/Google removed, signup creates leadership, signup lock + UI default to signin + no switch link, sign-in works, sidebar Home + empty states)

test_credentials: Created and CLEANED UP: first@studio.com / firstuser123 / 'First Owner' (leadership). DB is empty again at end of testing — verified via mongosh and GET /api/auth/signup-allowed=={allowed:true}.
seed_data_creation: Created a single transient user via POST /api/auth/signup (first@studio.com). Deleted via mongosh at end of run along with user_sessions. All 12 collections (users/projects/tasks/events/leaves/clients/teams/comments/time_entries/active_timers/notifications/user_sessions) are 0 documents at conclusion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Empty-DB + first-user signup iteration is GREEN. Note: backend EmailStr rejects '.local' TLDs — use *.com for any test fixtures. The legacy /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py will fail many cases by design (no seed users). To re-run just the signup tests: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_signup_flow.py -v -o addopts=''` (pytest.ini has fixed -n 2 --dist loadscope; the test module is module-scoped so xdist works fine too). The cleanup fixture deletes all users + sessions both before and after the module runs, so it's safe to re-run repeatedly.
rca of the issue: Single backend nit: Pydantic v2 EmailStr (backed by email-validator) treats .local as a special-use domain per RFC 6762/2606 and refuses it. The user's suggested test email 'first@studio.local' triggers HTTP 422. Repro: curl -X POST /api/auth/signup -d '{"name":"x","email":"a@b.local","password":"12345678"}' returns 422 with detail mentioning 'special-use or reserved name'. Mitigation: switch to a real-TLD email (used 'first@studio.com' in this run), or relax the EmailStr validator via custom field with allow_special_use=True.
